reasoning: The yield spread (T10YFF) comparison value on 2025-08-31 is effectively the 2025-08-29 value of -0.10%. By 2025-09-07, no Fed meetings will have occurred, so the EFFR will remain stable while 10-year yields are trending downwards in anticipation of incoming rate cuts. The FRED statistical momentum estimate gives a 37% chance of an increase by early September, and we adjust this slightly to 0.34. However, the FOMC is highly anticipated (87%+ probability) to cut rates by 25 bps on September 17. Once this happens, the EFFR drops mechanically, which immediately provides a ~+25 bps boost to the spread. As 10-year yields have largely already priced in this cut, they are unlikely to decline 1-for-1 with the EFFR, leading to a strong widening (un-inversion) of the yield curve by 2025-09-30 (p=0.64). Furthermore, maintaining the prompt's strict instruction ("uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5"), the probabilities for the longer horizons (Nov 29 and Feb 27) are scaled progressively back toward 0.5 (0.59 and 0.56 respectively). These reflect the structural un-inverting trend of the yield curve during cutting cycles but correctly apply the requested heuristic for horizon uncertainty.
